summary: Applied the private step-ca runtime live on docker-runtime-lv3, published it through the Proxmox host Tailscale path, and verified SSH and internal X.509 issuance end to end.

verification.observed: make converge-step-ca completed successfully from the main integration worktree after the compose path and certificate-chain verification fixes landed.
curl with the mirrored root certificate returned {"status":"ok"} from https://100.118.189.95:9443/health.
A locally issued ops SSH certificate successfully logged into debian-base-template over Tailscale and docker-runtime-lv3 through the Proxmox jump path.
A locally issued services certificate for local-step-ca-test.lv3.internal was minted through the proxied controller URL and inspected as issued by LV3 Internal CA Intermediate CA.

notes: The live rollout required aligning the compose mount layout with the absolute paths written by step ca init so the container could reuse the initialized host-side PKI directory.
The converge verification now validates service certificates against the intermediate chain because step-ca issues through an intermediate CA rather than directly from the root.
